results.title: Senior Security Documentation – Design Specialist at POOLCORP - United States Remote
highlights:
Senior Security Documentation – Design Specialist at POOLCORP - United States Remote
...
POOLCORP
...
# Senior Security Documentation – Design Specialist
...
POOLCORP is the world's largest wholesale distributor of swimming pool supplies, equipment, and related leisure products. Operating approximately 440 sales centers across North America, Europe, and Australia, the company distributes over 200,000 products to around 125,000 wholesale customers worldwide. POOLCORP's extensive inventory includes construction materials, pool care products, hot tubs, irrigation items, and various outdoor living products. The company is committed to sustainability and providing exceptional value by sourcing the best products at competitive prices while maximizing supply logistics.
...
### 📋 Description
...
• Ensure the Information Security organization speaks with one clear, consistent, and credible voice — in its writing, its visuals, and the artifacts that carry the program to the rest of the enterprise. • Design and produce Board-level and executive security materials — risk reports, posture dashboards, program updates, and decision briefs — translating technical findings into clear narratives and high-impact visuals for non-technical leadership. • Help design a unified security-metrics reporting layer in which a single, shared pool of metrics is surfaced at every altitude — operational dashboards for analysts, reporting for management, and narrative for executives and the Board. • Author and maintain security policies, standards, procedures, and control narratives, ensuring alignment with frameworks such as NIST CSF, PCI-DSS, and SOX. • Conceive, write, illustrate, and produce security awareness and training content — e-learning modules, microlearning, phishing-simulation creative, infographics, posters, and campaign materials. • Produce and maintain operational documentation for the security organization — runbooks, incident-response playbooks, the InfoSec operations manual, and executive incident summaries.
...
### 🎯 Requirements
...
• 5+ years of professional experience spanning two or more of: technical or visual communication, information/UX design, technical illustration, and editorial/copywriting — ideally in a corporate or enterprise setting. • Hands-on experience authoring security and compliance documentation — for example, PCI-DSS control documentation and audit evidence, security policies, or other audit-ready artifacts. • A strong portfolio demonstrating both polished visual design (diagrams, infographics, illustration, presentation design) and clear, well-structured writing. • Mastery of professional design tools (e.g., Adobe Illustrator, InDesign, Photoshop; Figma) and diagramming tools (e.g., Lucidchart, Visio, draw.io). • Ability to translate concepts into interactive wireframes and functional prototypes, using AI-assisted development tools to build working proofs of concept without a traditional engineering hand-off. • Comfort designing reporting and dashboard interfaces that draw from shared data sources, so the same metrics can be presented consistently across live dashboards, slides, web pages, and written formats. • Exceptional writing and editing ability, with a proven knack for making complex or dry material clear, accurate, and engaging for varied audiences. • Demonstrated ability to learn unfamiliar technical domains quickly by interviewing experts and reading source material independently. • Strong delivery discipline — the ability to turn open-ended, creative work into finished, published artifacts on a predictable schedule.
...
👮‍♂️ Cybersecurity / Security Engineer
...
☁️ SaaS
...
🤖 Artificial Intelligence
...
Software Engineer in Product Security developing security tooling and automation for a global organization in cybersecurity. Collaborate with teams to safeguard a multi-cloud production environment.

results.title: Pool Corporation AI Adoption Tracker | Larridin | AI Initiative Tracker
highlights:
Pool Corporation (POOLCORP) has limited visible AI initiatives compared to other major distributors, but shows emerging signals through strategic partnerships and operational improvements. The company announced a partnership with Aiper in September 2025 to distribute AI-powered cordless robotic pool cleaners through its 450+ location network[1]. POOLCORP has also invested in digital transformation with its POOL360 platform now accounting for 16% of total sales[2]. While the company selected ToolsGroup in 2021 to implement AI-powered demand planning and forecasting across its supply chain network[3], most of POOLCORP's AI exposure appears to come through distributing third-party AI-enabled products rather than developing proprietary AI solutions.
...
## AI Hiring Signals
...
Pool Corporation Job Postings Analysis
...
AI-Related Jobs
...
Non-Tech AI Requirements
...
Tech Roles (Engineering/Data) 0.0%
...
Non-Tech Roles 10.3%
...
Non-tech AI adoption exceeds tech roles - strong org-wide AI fluency signal
...
POOLCORP shows limited AI hiring signals with only 6.7% of job postings mentioning AI keywords. Interestingly, non-technical roles show higher AI expectations (10.3%) than technical roles (0%), suggesting AI is being integrated into business operations rather than core technology development. The highest AI mention rates are in HR (25%) and Sales (20%), indicating focus on AI tools for business processes rather than product development.

### Aiper Robotic Pool Cleaner Distribution
...
Strategic partnership to distribute Aiper's AI-powered cordless robotic pool cleaners through POOLCORP's global distribution network
...
Beginning in 2026, independent retailers, pool builders, and service companies will have access to Aiper's full suite of robotic pool cleaners, including exclusive products, through POOLCORP's 450+ locations. The partnership brings cognitive AI-powered pool cleaning technology to the professional trade market.
...
Digital ecosystem that enhances customer engagement and reduces reliance on traditional sales channels. Now accounts for 16% of total sales and serves as foundation for further digital innovations and potential AI integration.
...
POOLCORP distributes Aiper's cognitive AI-powered robotic pool cleaners starting in 2026, including the Scuba V3 series with features like dual-camera AI patrol cleaning and cognitive AI navigation.
...
POOLCORP implemented ToolsGroup's AI-powered demand planning and forecasting solution in 2021 to optimize inventory across over 1 million SKU/locations and improve supply chain resilience.
...
AI technology?
...
POOLCORP appears to focus primarily on distributing third-party AI-enabled products rather than developing proprietary AI solutions, though they have invested in AI-powered supply chain optimization tools.
...
POOLCORP's AI strategy is primarily distribution-focused, partnering with AI technology companies like Aiper rather than developing internal AI capabilities like some competitors.

results.title: Brent Sanders
highlights:
Cash Application Manager at POOLCORP with expertise in Credit Management and Technology Innovation
...
Leading the Cash Applications team at POOLCORP, my focus on technology innovations has enhanced operational efficiency. Through the use of AI, Machine Learning, and OCR I have been able to manage personnel and budgetary constraints as the company has seen significant growth and change. Skills in account management and data analysis are integral to my role, ensuring precise financial transactions and reliable reporting. Our successes have been marked by improved credit management systems and support services that cater to evolving industry standards. A dedicated professional, my career is characterized by a continuous pursuit of process improvements. With a solid track record in managing both consumer and commercial debt portfolios, I've been pivotal in integrating new debt portfolio purchases and resolving regulatory disputes. My contributions also extend to developing compliance policies and spearheading training initiatives, furthering the organization's commitment to best practices. Customer service and teamwork are areas in which I excel.

results.title: Darrell S.
highlights:
Director Of Pricing at [
...
VALUE PROPOSITION As a strategic Pricing & Data Transformation Leader with 28+ years of progressive experience in wholesale distribution, I specialize in building, governing, and optimizing pricing frameworks that drive measurable business impact. My expertise spans the full spectrum of pricing strategy— from analytics and automation to cross-functional team leadership and post-M&A integration.Currently serving as Director of Pricing at POOLCORP, the world’s largest distributor of pool and outdoor living products, I’ve led enterprise-wide pricing transformation initiatives, unifying strategic vision with practical execution. I excel at translating complex data into actionable insights using advanced analytics (SQL, Power BI) and have a proven track record of delivering operational efficiencies, margin growth, and scalable pricing solutions. Additionally, I have extensive experience leading master data management programs and large-scale data migrations, empowering organizations to make better, data-driven decisions.Key strengths include:- Building and governing global/regional pricing structures- Architecting and executing data consolidation and automation initiatives- Integrating and enriching master data post-acquisition- Leading teams through high-impact process transformation- Delivering executive storytelling and data-driven insights to align pricing strategy with business goals
...
### Director Of Pricing at [POOLCORP](
...
://www.linkedin.com/company/poolcorp) (Current)
...
★ Led the vision, strategy, and execution of pricing initiatives, managing four million customer price records through the Prelude ERP application. ★ Directed a team of four pricing analysts and five pricing managers, extracting and analyzing large data sets from multiple systems to support pricing decisions. ★ Standardized pricing structures across 62 markets, 300,000 products, and 300+ locations, enhancing operational efficiency. ★ Developed and delivered an analytics roadmap, driving business value and improving stakeholder visibility. ★ Built and maintained comprehensive metrics and KPIs to measure the health and success of the revenue organization. ★ Innovated and implemented automated pricing processes, increasing field capacity by 70% and reducing field office workload by 40%. ★ Contributed to post-acquisition integration by converting master data for products, vendors, customers, and pricing from acquired companies. ★ Conducted ad-hoc pricing studies and analyses to evaluate opportunities for pricing improvement and competitive positioning. ★ Developed and tested pricing hypotheses, presenting findings and recommendations to senior leadership, while managing related pricing data projects. ★ Collaborated with Finance and Accounting to provide insights and recommend best practices for pricing management. ★ Led the development of advanced pricing analytics strategies to drive growth and profitability. ★ Established processes for bench marking pricing against competitors, identifying short-term tactical pricing opportunities.
...
I began my career at POOLCORP on August 19, 1997, as a driver and steadily advanced through roles including Sales Associate, Operations Manager, and Assistant Branch Manager. My passion for data led me to the position of Lead Data Analyst, followed by Manager of Data, where I drove key projects in data management and integration. In August 2018, I was promoted to Corporate Pricing Manager, leading enterprise pricing strategy and optimization initiatives. My journey reflects a commitment to continuous growth and delivering business value across multiple facets of the organization.

results.title: Luther Willems
highlights:
I lead the human capital function for POOLCORP (Nasdaq: POOL), the world’s largest wholesale distributor of swimming pool and outdoor-living products with approximately 6,000 employees across 450+ sales centers in North America, Europe, and Australia, operating mainly through the SCP, Horizon, and Pinch A Penny brands. As Chief Human Resources Officer, my focus is on creating enterprise value through executive compensation and the board governance that surrounds it, executive succession and leadership transition, and a talent strategy built to perform across a seasonal, field-based, multi-jurisdictional business. I serve as the management lead to the Board’s Compensation & Human Capital Management Committee and chair the company’s 401 (k) committee. What I care about most is the connection between people decisions and how a business actually performs, designing compensation, performance, and development systems rigorous enough for a public company and individualized enough that people want to stay and grow. I came to HR by way of learning & development with an educational background in English literature, which is probably why I still believe most leadership problems are, at their heart, communication problems. Areas of focus: executive compensation design • board and committee governance • succession planning • total rewards and benefits • HR technology and people analytics (Workday) • talent acquisition • performance management • employee relations • multi-state and international HR compliance • M&A and integration.
...
### Vice President, Chief Human Resources Officer at [POOLCORP](https://www.linkedin.com/company/poolcorp) (Current)
...
Lead the human capital function for the world’s largest wholesale distributor of pool and outdoor-living products with ~6,000 employees across 450+ sales centers in North America, Europe, and Australia (SCP, Horizon, Pinch A Penny brands).
...
- Serve as management lead to the Board’s Compensation & Human Capital Management Committee, owning the governance cadence, materials, and executive compensation program design for a Nasdaq-listed company. • Chair the company’s 401(k) committee, overseeing plan governance and fiduciary oversight for the U.S. workforce.
...
- Supported executive leadership transitions and succession at the most senior levels. • Modernized the HR technology and people-analytics stack (Workday), giving leadership real-time visibility into headcount, vacancy, and talent data across 15+operating divisions; held enterprise vacancy rate at ~4.2%. • Redesigned enterprise recognition and performance programs into separate award tracks, tying reward more directly to business outcomes across a seasonal, field-based operating model.

results.title: Johnson Controls – Associate Software Engineer (Data Scientist) - Job Society
highlights:
Johnson Controls is seeking a talented and passionate Associate Software Engineer – Data Scientist to join its Data Science & AI team in Pune. This role is an excellent opportunity for aspiring data science professionals who want to work on advanced machine learning, artificial intelligence, and cloud-based analytics solutions. As part of a global technology-driven organization, you will contribute to building intelligent systems that transform complex business challenges into data-driven solutions. The position offers exposure to cutting-edge technologies including machine learning, deep learning, generative AI, cloud computing, and MLOps, making it an ideal opportunity for professionals looking to accelerate their careers in the rapidly evolving field of data science.
...
In this role, you will collaborate with data engineers, AI specialists, cloud architects, and business stakeholders to develop scalable machine learning models and AI-powered applications. You will work extensively with Python, SQL, Microsoft Azure, Azure Machine Learning, Azure Databricks, and Azure OpenAI services to create intelligent solutions that generate measurable business value. The position involves the complete machine learning lifecycle, including data preparation, feature engineering, model development, deployment, monitoring, and optimization. By leveraging advanced analytics and cloud-native technologies, you will help build predictive and automated systems that improve operational efficiency and business decision-making.
...
The Associate Software Engineer – Data Scientist role provides an exceptional platform for learning and innovation. Johnson Controls encourages continuous skill development through mentorship programs, certification sponsorships, and hands-on exposure to enterprise-scale AI and machine learning projects. You will gain experience working with modern technologies such as Generative AI, Natural Language Processing (NLP), Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), deep learning frameworks, and cloud-based MLOps solutions. This position is ideal for candidates who are eager to solve real-world business problems using data, artificial intelligence, and advanced analytics while contributing to transformative technology initiatives within a globally recognized organization.
...
• Design, develop, and evaluate machine learning models for classification, regression, forecasting, recommendation systems, and predictive analytics use cases.
...
• Utilize Azure OpenAI and other AI services to develop intelligent applications, automation solutions, and generative AI-powered business capabilities.
...
• Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Artificial
...
• Understanding of machine learning concepts including supervised learning, unsupervised learning, feature engineering, model evaluation, and hyperparameter tuning.
...
• Experience working with deep learning frameworks such as TensorFlow or PyTorch for developing AI and neural network-based solutions.
...
• Understanding of Natural Language Processing (NLP), large language models, Hugging Face libraries, spaCy, NLTK, or related AI technologies is advantageous.
...
• Knowledge of Generative AI, Azure OpenAI, LangChain, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), and prompt engineering concepts will be highly beneficial.
...
The expected salary for an Associate Software Engineer – Data Scientist at Johnson Controls in Pune typically ranges between ₹8 LPA and ₹16 LPA, depending on educational background, technical expertise, project experience, cloud certifications, and machine learning proficiency. Candidates with strong Azure cloud knowledge, hands-on AI and machine learning experience, deep learning expertise, and exposure to Generative AI technologies may receive compensation toward the higher end of the range, along with performance incentives, certification sponsorships, learning benefits, and long-term career growth opportunities.
